ddls:
CREATE TABLE "security_updates" (
  "update_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each security update */,
  "cve_id" TEXT /* CVE ID of the security update */,
  "product" TEXT /* Product affected by the security update */,
  "kb_article" TEXT /* Knowledge Base (KB) article associated with the update */,
  "severity" TEXT /* Severity level of the vulnerability (e.g., Critical, High, Medium, Low) */,
  "impact" TEXT /* Impact of the vulnerability (e.g., Remote Code Execution, Denial of Service) */,
  "superseded_by" TEXT /* ID of the security update that supersedes this one */,
  "cvss_base_score" REAL /* Base CVSS score for the vulnerability */,
  "cvss_temporal_score" REAL /* Temporal CVSS score for the vulnerability */,
  "cvss_vector" TEXT /* CVSS vector string */,
  "restart_required" INTEGER /* Indicates whether a system restart is required after applying the update */,
  "release_date" TEXT /* Date when the security update was released */,
  "description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the security update */,
  "patch_url" TEXT /* URL where the patch can be downloaded */,
  "patch_size" REAL /* Size of the patch file in MB */,
  "approved_by" TEXT /* Username of the user who approved the update */,
  "approval_date" TEXT /* Date when the update was approved */,
  "is_tested" INTEGER /* Boolean indicating whether the patch has been tested */,
  "test_date" TEXT /* Date when the patch was tested */,
  "test_result" TEXT /* Result of the patch testing (e.g., Passed, Failed) */,
  "is_emergency" INTEGER /* Boolean indicating whether the patch is an emergency update */,
  "notes" TEXT /* Any additional notes or comments about the update */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("update_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_security_updates_product FOREIGN KEY ("product") REFERENCES products ("product_name")
)

CREATE TABLE "products" (
  "product_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each product */,
  "product_name" TEXT /* Name of the product */,
  "product_version" TEXT /* Version of the product */,
  "platform" TEXT /* Platform on which the product runs (e.g., 32-bit, 64-bit, ARM64) */,
  "release_date" TEXT /* Date the product was released */,
  "vendor" TEXT /* Name of the vendor or manufacturer of the product */,
  "end_of_life_date" TEXT /* Date when the product reaches its end of life */,
  "support_contact" TEXT /* Contact information for support (e.g., email, phone) */,
  "is_supported" INTEGER /* Boolean indicating whether the product is currently supported */,
  "last_update_date" TEXT /* Date of the last security update applied to the product */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("product_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "update_logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each update log entry */,
  "update_id" INTEGER /* ID of the security update that was applied */,
  "system_id" TEXT /* ID of the system on which the update was applied */,
  "applied_date" TEXT /* Date the update was applied */,
  "applied_by" TEXT /* Username of the user who applied the update */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the update (e.g., Applied, Failed, Pending) */,
  "system_name" TEXT /* Name of the system on which the update was applied */,
  "system_location" TEXT /* Location of the system (e.g., data center, office) */,
  "system_os_version" TEXT /* Version of the operating system on the system */,
  "log_notes" TEXT /* Any additional notes or comments about the update log entry */,
  "is_automated" INTEGER /* Boolean indicating whether the update was applied automatically */,
  "error_message" TEXT /* Error message if the update failed */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_update_logs_update_id FOREIGN KEY ("update_id") REFERENCES security_updates ("update_id")
)
